Internet Folklore

Cicada 3301: Internet Puzzle, Cryptography, and the Mystery of an Unknown Group

Cicada 3301 is the name given to a series of anonymous internet puzzles that appeared in the early 2010s. The puzzles used cryptography, steganography, literature, music, Tor sites, and physical coordinates, turning an online challenge into a global hunt.

Internet Folklore - Internet Puzzle - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Internet Folklore

Herobrine Minecraft Legend: Origin, Meaning, and Gaming Folklore

A source-aware Kyunolab record tracing the impossible player figure said to appear inside a familiar block world and change how screenshots are read.

Internet Folklore - Gaming Folklore - 10 min read - Updated Jul 12, 2026
Internet Folklore

Jeff the Killer Creepypasta: The Smile, the Image, and an Internet Horror Legend

Jeff the Killer is a creepypasta figure known for a pale face, carved-looking smile, and the phrase go to sleep.

Internet Folklore - Creepypasta Legend - 9 min read - Updated Jul 20, 2026
Internet Folklore

Momo Challenge Rumor: How a Viral Warning Became Internet Folklore

A source-aware Kyunolab record tracing the online panic in which a disturbing image, child-safety fears, and reposted warnings formed a modern rumor cycle.

Internet Folklore - Viral Rumor - 11 min read - Updated Jul 12, 2026
Internet Folklore

Polybius Arcade Game Legend: The Missing Cabinet, Rumor, and Internet Folklore

Polybius is an internet folklore story about a mysterious arcade game supposedly tested in the early 1980s. The legend says the cabinet caused strange physical or psychological effects, then disappeared.

Internet Folklore - Arcade Game Legend - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026

A source-aware Kyunolab record tracing the rumored arcade cabinet that became a story about missing games, memory, and machine anxiety.

Russian Sleep Experiment Creepypasta: Fake Document, Laboratory Horror, and Viral Belief

Russian Sleep Experiment is a pre-existing Internet Folklore subject built around the creepypasta framed as a secret experiment in which prisoners are kept awake until the account becomes impossible.

Smile Dog Creepypasta: Haunted Image, Email Chain, and the Face People Shared

Ted the Caver: Cave Journal, Early Web Horror, and the Diary That Felt Found

Ted the Caver is a pre-existing Internet Folklore subject built around the early online horror story presented as a caving journal about a narrow passage and a presence below ground.

The App Crash Report That Described the Room

A source-aware Kyunolab record tracing a phone crash report includes a phrase that seems to describe the room around the device.

Internet Folklore

The Archived Forum Panel Register With a Line Written Too Early

The Archived Forum Panel Register With a Line Written Too Early follows a archived forum panel register places a reply number that appears before the first post one line earlier than the surrounding entries allow, showing how ordinary paperwork can become the center of a legend, mystery, or symbolic tradition.

Internet Folklore - Digital Record Folklore - 8 min read - Updated Jul 18, 2026
